<!-- Generated by scripts/generate_profile.py; refreshed nightly by .github/workflows/refresh-profile.yml.
     Keep the alt text on ONE line: a blank line inside a raw HTML block ends the block, which drops
     the <img> and leaves a <picture> that renders nothing at all.
     No width="100%" on the img. A percentage width stretches the panel to whatever the column is,
     which upscaled it 1.22x and read as zoomed. Bare intrinsic width renders 1:1 and can only ever
     shrink, via GitHub's own max-width:100%.
     The breakpoint is 720px, not 500px, because the desktop panel is drawn 1200px wide: a column
     narrower than that scales it down, and by ~700px the text is under 9px. Below the breakpoint
     the 460px narrow variant renders at its own size instead, which is legible. -->
<picture>
  <source media="(max-width: 720px) and (prefers-color-scheme: dark)" srcset="assets/profile-narrow-dark.svg">
  <source media="(max-width: 720px)" srcset="assets/profile-narrow-light.svg">
  <source media="(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" srcset="assets/profile-dark.svg">
  <img src="assets/profile-light.svg" alt="Terminal session. whoami: Dylan Fodor, Full-Stack Developer at Software Consulting Services. cat about.txt: I build and modernize the software newspapers use to sell and produce advertising. I lean front-end but work the whole stack, and the work I'm proudest of is the load-bearing kind: two-factor auth, payment processing, and a generative-AI tool that makes print-ready ad artwork. Reliability first, because that's the part people only notice when it's missing. ls ~/tools: argus, a call/impact graph for a million-line legacy codebase; aegis, a build-trust engine that compiles on the real host and never reports a false green; iris, headless visual verification over the Chrome DevTools Protocol; demeter, a dev-database harness; ariadne, a long-effort tracker; delphi, a personal knowledge engine with scored recall; momus, an adversarial reviewer that assumes my change is broken. tail shipped.log: multi-method two-factor auth live across 90+ newspaper sites; a provider-agnostic payment layer plus a processor migration; print-ready ad artwork generated from a text prompt; Google Ad Manager integration from order entry; a multi-tenant classified-ads marketplace built solo. tail activity.log: recent contribution counts, a 30-day trend, and the latest public push, refreshed nightly. cat stack.txt: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, PHP, SQL; React and Next.js; Node.js, Flask, Express, REST and JSON-RPC APIs; PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis; AWS, Docker, CI/CD, Linux; LLM integration, image generation, speech to text.">
</picture>
